title: Japan's Volcano Buried a City of 600,000 in Grey Rain
script: Grey rain? Sakurajima, Japan's most active volcano, erupted on June 7 and blanketed Kagoshima City in thick volcanic ash. When the ash mixed with light rainfall, it turned into grey mud that coated streets, cars, and rooftops across a city of 600,000 people. The eruption column reached 1,300 meters above the crater. Kagoshima Airport cancelled flights. Rail services halted. Residents were ordered to wear masks. The Japan Meteorological Agency maintained Volcanic Alert Level 3, restricting access near the summit. Sakurajima has erupted more than 15,000 times since 1955 and sits 5 kilometers from the city center. Locals live with regular ashfall as just a fact of life. Why would anyone choose to live next to the world's most active volcano?
